quick_facts.label: Recognised degree and minimum duration
quick_facts.value: The University Grants Commission’s specification of degrees lists B.Des. as “Bachelor of Design” with a minimum duration of four years.

quick_facts.label: School-leaving eligibility is institution-specific
quick_facts.value: At NID for academic year 2026–27, B.Des. applicants may have passed or be appearing in Class XII/equivalent in any stream, including Science, Arts, Commerce and Humanities. UCEED-participating institutes apply stream requirements that vary by institute.

quick_facts.label: Examples of specialisations
quick_facts.value: Official programmes include communication/graphic, industrial/product, furniture and interior, textile, film and video, animation, ceramic and glass design at NID; NIFT lists fashion, leather, accessory, textile, knitwear and fashion communication design.

quick_facts.label: Common selective admission routes
quick_facts.value: Major public-institute routes include NID’s two-stage Design Aptitude Test (DAT) and UCEED-based joint admission to participating IIT/IIITDM B.Des. programmes; NIFT publishes a separate annual admission prospectus and counselling process.

quick_facts.label: Postgraduate progression
quick_facts.value: A B.Des. can support applications to M.Des. programmes. For example, NID’s 2026–27 M.Des. handbook accepts a bachelor’s degree or equivalent in any discipline, while CEED requires a qualifying degree/diploma/postgraduate programme of at least three years after 10+2 and does not itself guarantee admission.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class XII is the usual entry point, but rules differ by institute
paragraphs: B.Des. is a four-year undergraduate degree. In the 2026–27 NID admission cycle, candidates could apply after passing or appearing for Class XII/equivalent in any stream from a recognised board; NID also set category-specific age limits and documentation rules. This is an example of institute-level policy, not a universal rule for every B.Des. provider.

For UCEED-based B.Des. admissions in 2026, applicants had to qualify UCEED and have passed all five subjects in Class XII/equivalent in 2025 or 2026. Stream access differed across participating institutes: candidates from all streams could apply to IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Hyderabad and IIT Indore, whereas IIT Guwahati and IIT Roorkee required Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics, and IIITDM Jabalpur required PCM or PCB.

Students should therefore check the current prospectus of each target institute rather than assuming that Arts & Humanities, Science, Commerce, NATA, or one entrance score automatically provides eligibility everywhere. In particular, NID’s official FAQ states that NATA scores are not considered for its entrance process; NID uses DAT.
bullets: NID 2026–27: Class XII/equivalent in any stream, subject to its cycle-specific age, result and document conditions.
UCEED route: eligibility combines a UCEED rank, recency of Class XII/equivalent, and institute-specific subject requirements.
NIFT and private/university B.Des. programmes may publish different rules; use their current prospectus before applying.

sections.heading: Duration and academic structure
paragraphs: The UGC specification lists B.Des. (Bachelor of Design) as a four-year degree. Programme structure is then set by the institution and specialisation. For example, NID Haryana describes a two-semester Foundation Programme followed by six semesters of specialised courses, while NID Assam describes a full-time four-year multidisciplinary B.Des. across Industrial, Communication, and Textile & Apparel Design streams.
bullets: Minimum duration in the UGC specification: four years.
A common institutional pattern is a foundation phase followed by disciplinary specialisation, but exact semester plans and credits vary.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: A B.Des. is not one uniform syllabus. Its curriculum is shaped by the design discipline selected and the provider’s academic model. NID’s published objectives emphasise experiential and exploratory learning, student-centred assignments, interdisciplinary knowledge, technical and managerial fundamentals, user- and culture-centred problem-solving, and professional responsibility.

Specialisation choices illustrate the breadth of the degree. NID Ahmedabad lists communication disciplines including graphic design, animation, exhibition design and film/video; industrial disciplines including product design, furniture/interior and ceramic/glass; and textile design. NIFT’s regular B.Des. offerings include fashion, leather, accessory, textile, knitwear and fashion communication design.
bullets: Foundation and design process: exploration, creative innovation and problem-solving.
Discipline learning: courses become more specialised in areas such as product, communication, textile, fashion or accessory design.
Professional preparation: curricula may integrate technical, managerial, social and industry-facing dimensions of design practice.

sections.heading: Admission: choose the institution first, then its applicable test and counselling process
paragraphs: There is no single national admission process covering all B.Des. seats. NID’s 2026–27 B.Des. process used DAT Prelims followed, for shortlisted candidates, by DAT Mains. The DAT Mains included a Studio Sensitivity Test and an In-Person Sensitivity Test; NID stated that DAT Prelims was used for shortlisting and that final merit used DAT Mains performance.

For the 2026–27 UCEED route, only UCEED-qualified students could submit the common B.Des. application for IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Guwahati, IIT Hyderabad, IIT Indore, IIT Roorkee and IIITDM Jabalpur. Seat allocation was based on All-India Rank, reservation category and seat availability, followed by admitting-institute document verification and fee formalities.

NIFT runs its own annual admissions process. Its official admissions page published the 2026 prospectus, entrance results, e-counselling and seat-allotment notices, so applicants should use that official page and the relevant year’s prospectus rather than relying on historical test formats or deadlines.
bullets: NID: online application → DAT Prelims → shortlisting → DAT Mains → merit/counselling, subject to the annual handbook.
UCEED: qualify the exam → submit the separate joint B.Des. application → participate in seat allocation → complete institute verification.
NIFT: follow the current NIFT/NTA prospectus, examination notices and counselling instructions.

sections.heading: Costs: distinguish application charges from the full cost of study
paragraphs: Fees are institution-, category-, campus- and year-specific. They should be verified from the provider’s current fee notice because application charges, tuition, hostel, materials, deposits and other charges are separate items.

As dated 2026-cycle examples, NID’s B.Des. application fee was ₹3,000 for Indian General/Gen-EWS/OBC-NCL applicants, ₹2,000 for female candidates except SC/ST/PwD, ₹1,500 for SC/ST applicants and ₹500 for PwD and third-gender applicants; the handbook stated that these charges excluded applicable bank/convenience charges and were non-refundable. For UCEED’s 2026 B.Des. joint-admission application, the counselling processing fee was ₹4,000 for all categories and genders, also non-refundable and non-transferable. These are application/counselling charges, not the four-year programme fee.
bullets: Do not treat an entrance or counselling fee as tuition.
Before accepting a seat, obtain the institute’s current fee schedule and identify tuition, hostel, mess, materials, refundable deposits and scholarship/financial-aid terms separately.

sections.heading: After B.Des.: design practice, M.Des., management or public-service pathways
paragraphs: The most direct next step is professional design practice in a role aligned with the student’s specialisation and portfolio—for example, product/industrial, graphic/communication, fashion, textile, accessory or UI/UX-related work. The exact role title and hiring criteria are employer-specific; the degree’s specialisation, project work and portfolio are especially relevant to this route.

For advanced design study, M.Des. is a common option. NID’s 2026–27 handbook accepts a bachelor’s degree or equivalent in any discipline for M.Des., subject to its other criteria. CEED is an important examination route for several postgraduate design programmes: candidates need a minimum three-year post-10+2 qualifying programme, but qualifying CEED does not guarantee admission because institutes set additional application and selection requirements.

A B.Des. also meets the basic degree-level foundation for many MBA and civil-services routes, subject to each programme’s rules. For example, IIM Ahmedabad’s 2026–28 MBA process required a recognised bachelor’s degree and a valid CAT-2025 score among its requirements. UPSC separately publishes the Civil Services Examination notification and schedule each year; candidates should always use the live notification for eligibility and dates.
bullets: M.Des. and CEED: pursue deeper specialisation, research or advanced design training; verify each institute’s portfolio, test and interview requirements.
MBA and CAT: an option for design management, product/business roles or entrepreneurship; admissions remain programme-specific.
UPSC Civil Services Examination: a separate competitive route after graduation, governed by the annual UPSC notification.
Employment: target roles through a strong portfolio and relevant specialisation; examples include product, graphic/communication, fashion, textile and accessory design.
